title: Hypothyroidism
root_mechanism: Autoimmune (Hashimoto's) or iodine/selenium deficiency → impaired T4 synthesis and/or T4→T3 peripheral conversion → low cellular thyroid hormone → reduced basal metabolic rate, slowed gut motility, fluid retention, and mitochondrial dysfunction.

interventions.intervention: Cold shower (start 30 sec, build to 2 min) focusing cold water on neck/thyroid region
interventions.physiological_effect: Cold stimulates thyroid gland directly via increased TSH release from pituitary; norepinephrine activates thyroid hormone synthesis pathway

interventions.intervention: Neck/thyroid area Abhyanga with warm castor oil + gentle thyroid massage (circular)
interventions.physiological_effect: Castor oil ricinoleic acid reduces local thyroid inflammation (anti-Hashimoto's); manual massage increases thyroid blood flow → better iodine delivery

interventions.intervention: Nadi Shodhana + Ujjayi pranayama (ocean breath)
interventions.physiological_effect: Ujjayi creates mild heat in thoracic region overlying thyroid; vibrational stimulation of thyroid via laryngeal contraction

interventions.intervention: Selenium-rich breakfast: Brazil nuts (2) + eggs + whole grain toast with coconut oil
interventions.physiological_effect: Selenium is essential cofactor for deiodinase enzymes (T4→T3 conversion); 2 Brazil nuts provide daily selenium requirement

interventions.intervention: Warm castor oil abdominal pack (liver area)
interventions.physiological_effect: Stimulates hepatic T4 to T3 conversion (70% of T3 is made in liver); castor oil enhances lymphatic detoxification of reverse T3

interventions.intervention: Ashwagandha decoction (500 mg in warm water)
interventions.physiological_effect: Withanolides in ashwagandha stimulate thyroid hormone synthesis; adaptogenic effect normalises HPA-HPT axis crosstalk

interventions.intervention: Iodine + selenium + zinc rich meal: seaweed, eggs, legumes, pumpkin seeds, wholegrains (cooked, not raw cruciferous)
interventions.physiological_effect: Iodine = thyroid hormone substrate; zinc required for TSH receptor function; avoid raw goitrogenic vegetables

interventions.intervention: 20-min brisk walk or light resistance exercise
interventions.physiological_effect: Exercise increases T3 receptor sensitivity in muscle; stimulates deiodinase activity; raises metabolic rate to compensate hypothyroid slowing

interventions.intervention: Warm sesame oil full-body massage (Sarvanga Abhyanga)
interventions.physiological_effect: Stimulates peripheral T3 receptor uptake; increases circulation to sluggish tissues; reduces myxoedematous fluid accumulation

interventions.intervention: Yoga sequence (neck sequence — see below)
interventions.physiological_effect: Neck postures directly stimulate thyroid gland mechanically; inversions increase blood supply to thyroid

interventions.intervention: Contrast neck bath (alternating hot and cold compress on neck)
interventions.physiological_effect: Vascular pumping in thyroid region → flushes inflammatory cytokines; stimulates thyroid cell metabolism

interventions.intervention: Dinner: easy-to-digest — moong dal soup, cooked vegetables, small amount grain
interventions.physiological_effect: Hypothyroid gut is sluggish — well-cooked, warm foods prevent constipation and bacterial overgrowth

interventions.intervention: Warm full-body bath with Himalayan pink salt (iodine source) + ginger powder
interventions.physiological_effect: Transdermal iodine absorption; ginger raises body temperature offsetting hypothyroid thermogenesis deficit

interventions.intervention: Cold neck shower
Thyroid Abhyanga (castor oil)
Warm castor oil liver pack
Contrast neck bath
Himalayan salt bath
Yoga (neck sequence)
Ashwagandha

relapse_rules.trigger: Symptoms return (fatigue, cold intolerance, weight gain)

relapse_rules.action: Increase anti-inflammatory interventions; add turmeric protocol

dietary_protocol.eat_window: 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM (no strict IF — hypothyroid metabolism already suppressed)
dietary_protocol.meal_order: Protein → Cooked vegetables → Complex carbs (cooked, never raw cruciferous)

fasting_rules.specification: 3 meals/day mandatory — skipping meals suppresses already-slow metabolic rate further
fasting_rules.physiological_effect: Regular meals maintain thyroid hormone demand; prolonged fasting → T3 conversion drops → worsens hypothyroid state

fasting_rules.specification: Take thyroxine medication 30–60 min before breakfast on empty stomach (if prescribed)
fasting_rules.physiological_effect: Food (especially calcium, iron, fibre) reduces thyroxine absorption by 20–40%; empty stomach maximises bioavailability

fasting_rules.specification: 2 Brazil nuts on waking — before any food
fasting_rules.physiological_effect: Selenium is rate-limiting cofactor for type 1 deiodinase (T4→T3 conversion); morning timing maximises cofactor availability for daytime thyroid hormone synthesis

fasting_rules.specification: Cook all cruciferous vegetables thoroughly; no raw broccoli/cabbage/cauliflower
fasting_rules.physiological_effect: Heat deactivates glucosinolates (goitrogenic compounds) that inhibit thyroid peroxidase and iodine uptake
Withanolides stimulate thyroid hormone synthesis and reduce cortisol-mediated HPT axis suppression; timing with food reduces GI side effects

fasting_rules.specification: No eating after 7 PM; resume at 7 AM
fasting_rules.physiological_effect: Gentle fast allows GH secretion overnight → supports metabolic rate; avoids aggressive IF that worsens T3 conversion

phase_macros.carbohydrates: Moderate: 45–50% of calories (low-GI, fibre-rich; cooked vegetables; millets)
phase_macros.protein: Moderate-high: 25–30% / 1.2–1.5 g/kg BW/day (eggs, fish, legumes — selenium + iodine sources)
phase_macros.fat: Moderate: 25–30% (coconut oil prioritised — stimulates T4→T3 conversion; sesame oil for massage AND cooking)
phase_macros.rationale: Support thyroid hormone synthesis via micronutrient-dense diet; protein raises T3 demand on tissues; coconut oil's MCTs stimulate conversion enzymes; avoid all goitrogens

phase_macros.carbohydrates: Moderate: 45–50% (introduce more variety; still avoid raw cruciferous)
phase_macros.fat: Moderate: 25–30% (maintain coconut oil; add fatty fish 3×/week)
phase_macros.rationale: Sustained thyroid support; increase selenium/iodine diversity; gentle caloric increase to support recovering metabolic rate
Lifelong selenium and iodine sufficiency; anti-Hashimoto's diet (gluten-reduced if autoimmune confirmed); maintain metabolic rate via regular exercise

items: Brazil nuts — 2/day (do not exceed; selenium toxicity possible)
Dairy — low-fat milk and curd (iodine from feed)
Bone broth (glycine supports gut integrity — leaky gut drives Hashimoto's)
Cooked spinach (iron — cofactor for thyroid peroxidase)
Jowar in large amounts (moderate amounts fine when cooked)
Excess caffeine (raises cortisol → suppresses HPT axis)

meals.physiological_rationale: Brazil nuts provide daily selenium requirement for T4→T3 deiodinase enzymes; warm water on empty stomach before thyroid medication

meals.what_to_eat: Thyroxine medication on empty stomach — wait 30–60 min before eating
meals.physiological_rationale: Empty stomach absorption of thyroxine maximised; calcium, iron, fibre in food reduce absorption 20–40%

meals.what_to_eat: 2 whole eggs (scrambled in coconut oil) + whole grain toast (small) + 1 tsp pumpkin seeds + ginger tea
meals.physiological_rationale: Eggs: selenium + iodine + zinc; coconut oil MCTs stimulate hepatic T4→T3 conversion; ginger raises body temperature offsetting hypothyroid thermogenesis deficit

meals.what_to_eat: Ashwagandha decoction (500 mg in warm water) + 1 cup warm water
meals.physiological_rationale: Withanolides stimulate thyroid synthesis; pre-lunch timing allows absorption before meal nutrients

meals.what_to_eat: Brown rice (small) or ragi roti + cooked dal + cooked spinach sabzi (iron) + nori sheet (iodine) + curd (selenium + probiotic)
meals.physiological_rationale: All cruciferous cooked — glucosinolates deactivated by heat; nori provides dietary iodine substrate; curd provides selenium + gut microbiome support (gut dysbiosis drives Hashimoto's)

meals.what_to_eat: Sunflower seeds (1 tbsp) + warm turmeric milk (no sugar)
meals.physiological_rationale: Sunflower seeds: selenium + zinc; turmeric curcumin reduces anti-TPO antibodies (anti-Hashimoto's); warm milk provides iodine

meals.what_to_eat: Moong dal khichdi (well-cooked, warm) + cooked vegetables (pumpkin, sweet potato) + 1 tsp ghee
meals.physiological_rationale: Hypothyroid gut is sluggish — well-cooked easy foods; sweet potato iodine and beta-carotene; ghee butyrate supports gut integrity (leaky gut perpetuates Hashimoto's)

meals.what_to_eat: Warm Himalayan salt bath (20 min) — then sleep by 10 PM
meals.physiological_rationale: Transdermal iodine absorption from Himalayan salt; sleep is critical — GH peaks in early sleep → supports thyroid recovery

functional_foods.name: Brazil nuts
functional_foods.dose_timing: 2 nuts daily — on waking before food (do not exceed 4/day)
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Highest dietary selenium source; selenium is obligatory cofactor for type 1, 2, and 3 deiodinase enzymes governing T4→T3 conversion; reduces thyroid inflammation in Hashimoto's

functional_foods.name: Ashwagandha (Withania somnifera)
functional_foods.dose_timing: 500 mg standardised extract in warm water — 20 min before lunch, supervised
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Withanolides stimulate thyroid hormone synthesis (raises T4 and T3); adaptogenic — normalises HPA-HPT axis crosstalk; reduces cortisol-driven TSH suppression

functional_foods.name: Seaweed/Kelp (nori)
functional_foods.dose_timing: 1–2 nori sheets — 2×/week in meals (not more — iodine excess also harmful)
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Dietary iodine is the primary substrate for thyroid hormone (T3 = 3 iodine atoms, T4 = 4); deficiency is primary cause of hypothyroidism globally

functional_foods.name: Coconut oil
functional_foods.dose_timing: 1–2 tbsp in cooking daily (replaces other oils)
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Lauric and caprylic acid (MCTs) stimulate hepatic type 1 deiodinase activity → increases T4→T3 peripheral conversion; raises basal metabolic rate 5% counteracting hypothyroid BMR depression

functional_foods.name: Pumpkin seeds
functional_foods.dose_timing: 1 tbsp daily — as snack or sprinkled on meals
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Rich in zinc — essential cofactor for TSH receptor function; also provides selenium and tryptophan supporting mood in hypothyroid-associated depression

functional_foods.name: Ginger (Zingiber officinale)
functional_foods.dose_timing: 1-inch fresh piece in warm water — morning; freely add to cooking
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: 6-Gingerol raises core body temperature (thermogenic) compensating hypothyroid deficit; reduces TNF-α and IL-6 in Hashimoto's thyroiditis; improves gut motility (hypothyroid constipation)

functional_foods.name: Turmeric
functional_foods.dose_timing: 1 tsp with black pepper in warm milk (no sugar) — evening
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Curcumin reduces anti-TPO antibody production by downregulating Th1/Th17 immune response; reduces NF-kB inflammation in thyroid gland; piperine increases bioavailability 2000%

functional_foods.name: Guggul (Commiphora mukul)
functional_foods.dose_timing: 250–500 mg standardised extract — with lunch, clinician supervised
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Guggulsterones directly stimulate thyroid function by increasing iodine uptake; activate thyroid peroxidase enzyme; documented to increase T3 levels by 30% in 4 weeks

asanas.physiological_effect: Direct pressure on thyroid gland → mechanical stimulation of hormone release; increased blood supply to thyroid via venous drainage reversal
Maximum neck arch exposes and stretches thyroid gland; maximises blood supply to thyroid; counter-pose to shoulder stand
Chin-lock (Jalandhara bandha) stimulates thyroid; compresses anterior neck → mechanically activates thyroid cell metabolism
Neck extension stimulates thyroid and parathyroid simultaneously; opens anterior chest improving respiratory exchange
Jalandhara bandha — classic thyroid toning posture; chin pressed to chest stimulates thyroid plexus
Tongue protrusion + neck stretch stimulates thyroid + larynx; increases circulation to anterior neck region
Ujjayi laryngeal vibration mechanically stimulates thyroid; Bhramari calms Hashimoto's autoimmune response via vagal-mediated immune modulation
Parasympathetic dominance; reduces autoimmune Th1 cytokine activity; allows thyroid tissue recovery

red_flags.action: Myxoedema coma risk — immediate hospital admission
Severe hypothyroid cardiac depression — urgent care

red_flags.sign: Chest pain or pericardial effusion symptoms

red_flags.action: Rule out hypothyroid ileus — surgical emergency

clinical_disclaimer: This protocol is designed to be implemented under the supervision of a qualified BNYS physician. Patients on thyroxine medication must not adjust doses without physician oversight. Ashwagandha and guggul require clinician supervision due to potential interaction with thyroid medications. This document is for clinical reference only.
